file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16-AMENDMENT TO THE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT BETWEEN INGRAM MICRO AND NETGEAR.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, including its attachment and order acknowledgments under the Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between Distributor and NETGEAR with respect to the purchase, resale and distribution of the Products and is governed by the laws of the State of California except that body of law dealing with conflicts of law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement, including its attachment and order acknowledgments under the Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between Distributor and NETGEAR with respect to the purchase, resale and distribution of the Products. The parties agree that the then current NETGEAR list price is confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause to a different part makes it harder to find and enforce.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1646 states that a contract is to be interpreted according to the law and usage of the place where it is to be performed, or, if it indicates a place of performance, according to the law and usage of the place where it is made. By omitting the governing law clause from its standard location, the contract creates ambiguity as to which jurisdiction's laws apply, potentially contradicting California law if the place of performance is unclear or outside California and the parties intend California law to govern.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1646 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1646. A contract is to be interpreted according to the law and usage of the place where it is to be performed; or, if it does not indicate a place of performance, according to the law and usage of the place where it is made. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the governing law as that of California, excluding conflict of law rules. The changed text removes this critical clause, potentially leading to disputes over which jurisdiction's laws apply. The LLM's law explanation and Perturbation Explanation highlights this ambiguity. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically CIV 1646, states that a contract should be interpreted according to the law of the place of performance or where it is made, but the removal of the governing law clause introduces uncertainty that could contradict the intent of this law if the place of performance is unclear or outside California. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, as a required legal disclosure is affected by the structural change.

perturbation.original_text: NETGEAR and Distributor having previously entered into a Distributor Agreement ("Agreement") with an Effective Date of March 1,1996, now mutually agree to amend that Agreement as follows: 1. Subject to the terms of this Amendment, the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States and [*].
perturbation.changed_text: NETGEAR and Distributor having previously entered into a Distributor Agreement ("Agreement") with an Effective Date of March 1,1996, now mutually agree to amend that Agreement as follows: NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at discounts to be determined at that time.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the territory clause to a different part makes it harder to find and enforce.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-306 requires good faith in output, requirements and exclusive dealing contracts. By obscuring the territory clause, it becomes unclear what the distributor's exclusive territory is, or if it is exclusive, leading to potential bad faith practices that would be difficult to legally challenge. The modified placement makes the area of exclusivity less clear. This is in conflict with the original agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. (1) A term which measures the quantity by the output of the seller or the requirements of the buyer means such actual output or requirements as may occur in good faith , except that no quantity unreasonably disproportionate to any stated estimate or in the absence of a stated estimate to any normal or otherwise comparable prior output or requirements may be tendered or demanded. (2) A lawful agreement by either the seller or the buyer for exclusive dealing in the kind of goods concerned imposes unless otherwise agreed an obligation by the seller to use best efforts to supply the goods and by the buyer to use best efforts to promote their sale . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The altered text presents a structural flaw that leads to a legal contradiction. The original contract text explicitly states that the 'Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States and [*].' whereas the changed text moves this clause and introduces the provision that 'NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion'. The Perturbation Explanation states the altered text makes it 'harder to find and enforce.' Also, the Law Explanation states that 'By obscuring the territory clause, it becomes unclear what the distributor's exclusive territory is, or if it is exclusive, leading to potential bad faith practices that would be difficult to legally challenge.' As per the Perturbation Definitions: 'Structural flaws occur when the organization of a contract affects its clarity or enforceability. A legal contradiction in this category arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.'
